findings: Functional spec complete: 10-row behavior table (B-1..B-10) covering populated listing, empty state, missing/corrupt manifests, artifact filtering, halted and defect-scope stories, unreadable state dir, and single-story regression guard.
Corrupt manifest policy decided: skip-and-mark (B-5) — emit { story_id, error } entry and continue; aborting would hide every healthy story's status behind one bad file.
Exit-code rule (BR-1): status --all always exits 0 except when .keel/state/ itself is unreadable (B-9); per-story problems are data, not failures — satisfies AC-2 fresh-repo semantics.

Field contract (BR-2): --all entries carry exactly story_id, scope (default 'feature'), current_phase, halted; deep fields (attempts, completed_phase_files, sequencing_gaps, timestamps) stay single-story-only per AC-1.
Interception point confirmed against dispatcher (keel-state.cjs:649-658): '--all' arrives as the storyId argument, so the branch must run before readManifest can die on a literal '--all' lookup.
--all is read-only (BR-3): no snapshot, no audit-log append, no manifest write; output sorted by story_id (BR-5) for stable health-report diffs.
Regression guard (BR-4/AC-3): single-story cmdStatus path untouched including exit-1 on missing manifest and sequencing gaps; engine suite must stay green.
No open questions and no payment/auth/PII surfaces; live fixture (KEEL-101, KEEL-102) gives a ready two-entry test case.
decisions: Corrupt manifest: skip-and-mark chosen over abort — health sweeps surface breakage as data rather than failing the whole listing
Sequencing-gap evaluation excluded from --all mode; it remains a single-story status concern so the exit-0 guarantee stays simple
Entries sorted lexicographically by story_id for deterministic output
'--all' treated as a reserved id intercepted before manifest lookup; a directory literally named --all is out of contract
AC-1, AC-2, AC-3 carried forward unchanged from 01-business-analyst.json
